item.title: Iran Hacktivists Make Noise but Have Little Impact on War
item.description:
Iran-aligned groups are trying to make their mark in the Gulf, but the results have fallen short of remarkable.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ex-NSA Directors Discuss 'Red Line' for Offensive Cyberattacks
item.description:
Four former NSA chiefs representing a near-complete history of US Cyber Command debated and discussed the role of offensive cyber in the government.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI-Native Security Is a Must to Counter AI-Based Attacks
item.description:
Attacks by artificial intelligence agents are a reality. Experts at Nvidia's GTC conference say defenders need to use the same tools to fight them off.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Why a 'Near Miss' Database Is Key to Improving Information Sharing
item.description:
Organizations disclose attack details, though information may be limited, following a breach, but what if they did the same with close calls?
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: SANS: Top 5 Most Dangerous New Attack Techniques to Watch
item.description:
For the first time, SANS Institute's five top attack techniques all have one thing in common — AI.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Blame Game: Why Public Cyber Attribution Carries Risks
item.description:
Publicly accusing an entity of a cyberattack could have negative consequences that organizations should consider before taking the plunge.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: DoE Publishes 5-Year Energy Security Plan
item.description:
CESER’s Project Armor is a five year initiative to harden the US critical energy infrastructure, including strengthening energy systems ‘to prevent and recover from wildfires and other hazards’. The post DoE Publishes 5-Year Energy Security Plan appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: HackerOne Employee Data Exposed in Massive Navia Breach
item.description:
The cybersecurity firm said the personal information of hundreds of employees was stolen in the hacker attack targeting Navia. The post HackerOne Employee Data Exposed in Massive Navia Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: US Prisons Russian Access Broker for Aiding Ransomware Attacks
item.description:
Aleksei Volkov has been sentenced to 81 months in prison for his role in Yanluowang ransomware attacks. The post US Prisons Russian Access Broker for Aiding Ransomware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: From Trivy to Broad OSS Compromise: TeamPCP Hits Docker Hub, VS Code, PyPI
item.description:
The hackers compromised GitHub Action tags, then shifted to NPM, Docker Hub, VS Code, and PyPI, and teamed with Lapsus$. The post From Trivy to Broad OSS Compromise: TeamPCP Hits Docker Hub, VS Code, PyPI appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: FCC Bans New Routers Made Outside the US Over National Security Risks
item.description:
The ban aligns with a White House determination that all routers produced abroad are a threat to national security. The post FCC Bans New Routers Made Outside the US Over National Security Risks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: iOS, macOS 26.4 Roll Out With Fresh Security Patches
item.description:
Apple released security fixes for older devices as well, in iOS 18.7.7, iPadOS 18.7.7, macOS Sequoia 15.7.5, and macOS Sonoma 14.8.5. The post iOS, macOS 26.4 Roll Out With Fresh Security Patches appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Speeds Attacks, But Identity Remains Cybersecurity’s Weakest Link
item.description:
PwC finds AI is amplifying speed and scale of attacks, as identity theft evolves into a cybercriminal supply chain. The post AI Speeds Attacks, But Identity Remains Cybersecurity’s Weakest Link appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russian Cybercriminal Gets 2-Year Prison Sentence in US
item.description:
Ilya Angelov was a member of the cybercrime group tracked as TA-551, Shathak, Gold Cabin, Monster Libra, and ATK236. The post Russian Cybercriminal Gets 2-Year Prison Sentence in US appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Onit Security Raises $11 Million for Exposure Management Platform
item.description:
The startup will invest in product development and go-to-market efforts as it expands into new sectors. The post Onit Security Raises $11 Million for Exposure Management Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russian botnet operator linked to major ransomware attacks sentenced in US
item.description:
Ilya Angelov, 40, of Tolyatti, Russia, pleaded guilty to managing a botnet that other cybercriminals used to break into corporate systems and deploy ransomware.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Puerto Rico government agency cancels driver’s license appointments after cyberattack
item.description:
Services at Centros de Servicios al Conductor (CESCO) — the agency responsible for issuing licenses, permits and vehicle registrations in Puerto Rico — cancelled all appointments due to a cyber incident.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ransomware attack disrupts operation at major Spanish fishing port
item.description:
A ransomware attack has disrupted digital systems at Spain’s Port of Vigo, forcing authorities to disconnect parts of its network and temporarily manage cargo operations manually.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Supply chain attack hits widely-used AI package, risks impacting thousands of companies
item.description:
The incident highlights growing concerns over the security of the open-source software supply chain, where widely-used tools maintained by small teams can provide a gateway into thousands of organizations if compromised.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA's acting chief warns shutdown is increasing cyber risks, causing resignations
item.description:
With CISA’s reduced capacity during the shutdown, Andersen said, the agency is largely limited to responding to imminent threats, protecting life and property, sharing critical vulnerability and incident information and keeping its 24/7 operation center up and running.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Florida Medicare members’ data exposed as Mirra Health improperly outsourced records overseas
item.description:
Skyler Shepard reports: State investigators say Mirra Health jeopardized the safety of thousands of Floridians by sharing their sensitive health data with unauthorized companies overseas. Florida Insurance Commissioner Mike Yaworsky suspended Mirra Health Care LLC on Tuesday after investigators found the company sent private medical information to unlicensed companies in India and the Philippines. Mirra Health handles important claims... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Infinite Campus Security Incident Awareness: No Impact to Student Data According to Infinite Campus
item.description:
There was some understandable panic and concern in the k-12 sector when the ShinyHunters threat actors announced they had compromised and would leak data from edtech vendor Infinite Campus. Was this going to be another massive breach like the PowerSchool breach that affected tens of millions of students? At the present time, the Infinite Campus... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Handala Hackers Alleges Massive Data Breach of Tamir Pardo, Former Mossad Chief
item.description:
As posted by the Iranian news agency, WANA: The hacker group Handala announced that it has released 14 gigabytes of personal and highly confidential documents belonging to Tamir Pardo as proof of concept (PoC). A message from Handala that accompanies some screengrabs offered as proof of claims states, in part: Today, Handala proudly announces that... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Anime streaming giant Crunchyroll says hacker stole data related to customer service tickets
item.description:
Jonathan Greig reports: The popular anime streaming platform Crunchyroll confirmed on Monday evening that a batch of customer information leaked online over the weekend is legitimate. In a statement to Recorded Future News, a spokesperson for the company said their investigation into the stolen documents is ongoing alongside cybersecurity experts. “At this time, we believe... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Malware on Luxembourg public sector devices was active for almost a month
item.description:
Alex Stevensson reports: Thousands of devices owned by the Luxembourg public sector found to be infected with malware at the end of February have since been updated and secured, digitalisation minister Stéphanie Obertin has said. The security breach was confirmed on 27 February but details were scant at the time, with LSAP deputy Ben Polidori... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Utimaco Survey Finds 78% of US Companies Name Legacy Data Breaches the Top Gen AI Risk
item.description:
From a press release by Utimaco: 57% of respondents have not yet implemented a solution to address these concerns Post quantum cryptography (PQC) readiness shows an even wider gap: while 72% identify legacy data attacks as the greatest quantum issue, 75% remain unprepared Data Sovereignty is front of mind: 80% of respondents rank protecting customer... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Companies face difficult choices in blaming hackers for an attack
item.description:
Eric Geller reports: Businesses need to think carefully about when they publicly blame a threat actor for a cyberattack, lest they invite unwanted consequences, experts said at a panel at the RSAC 2026 Conference here on Tuesday. “The rush to attribute is a risky one,” Megan Stifel, the chief strategy officer at the Institute for... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: TeamPCP Hits Trivy, Checkmarx, and LiteLLM in Credential Theft Campaign
item.description:
Deeba Ahmed reports on some of TeamPCP’s dangerously effective recent activities: What Happened? The trouble began on 19 March 2026, when a hacking group calling themselves TeamPCP managed to break into Trivy, a popular tool used by developers to scan their code for security vulnerabilities. This was a supply chain attack, which occurs when hackers sneak malicious code... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Delaware Supreme Court Reverses, Holds Cyber Insurers Sufficiently Pled Collective Subrogation Claim Resulting from Blackbaud Data Breach
item.description:
The fallout from the massive Blackbaud breach is not over, it seems. Lydia Mills of Wiley Rein writes: Reversing the decision below, the Delaware Supreme Court held that a group of cyber liability insurers sufficiently pled a complaint for subrogation based on breach of contract. Travelers Cas. & Sur. Co. of Am. v. Blackbaud, Inc., 2026... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Florida senator sues Booz Allen over his leaked tax returns
item.description:
Gary Fineout reports: Sen. Rick Scott is suing a major government contractor for damages after his tax returns were leaked along with other prominent and wealthy figures, including President Donald Trump. The Florida Republican on Monday filed a lawsuit against Booz Allen Hamilton, a management and technology consulting company, and a former employee of the contractor who... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Balance Autism Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
Balance Autism has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit stemming from a security incident that exposed patient information. Altoona, […] The post Balance Autism Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Business Associate HIPAA Checklist
item.description:
As aBusiness Associate, it is important to be aware of which HIPAA compliance standards apply to your organization. Do you […] The post Business Associate HIPAA Checklist appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: CMS Releases Final Rule Implementing HIPAA Standards for Health Care Claims Attachments
item.description:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) released a final rule […] The post CMS Releases Final Rule Implementing HIPAA Standards for Health Care Claims Attachments appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: National Association on Drug Abuse Problems Announces Data Breach Affecting 90,000 Individuals
item.description:
The National Association on Drug Abuse Problems has experienced a data breach affecting up to 90,000 individuals. An insider data […] The post National Association on Drug Abuse Problems Announces Data Breach Affecting 90,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Telehealth Platform Provider OpenLoop Health Disclosed Data Breach
item.description:
A major data breach has been reported by the telehealth platform provider OpenLoop Health Inc. While the total number of […] The post Telehealth Platform Provider OpenLoop Health Disclosed Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Florida Insurance Commissioner Suspends Mirra Health for Medicare Data Transfers to Foreign Companies
item.description:
The sensitive data of more than 23,000 Florida Medicare members has been impermissibly shared with overseas companies, putting Medicare members’ […] The post Florida Insurance Commissioner Suspends Mirra Health for Medicare Data Transfers to Foreign Companies appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Deaconess Health System Affected by Vendor Data Breach
item.description:
Evansville, Indiana-based Deaconess Health System has announced a data breach involving information shared with a third-party vendor, the MRO Corp-owned […] The post Deaconess Health System Affected by Vendor Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Manager of botnet used in ransomware attacks gets 2 years in prison
item.description:
A Russian national has been sentenced to two years in prison after admitting that the phishing botnet he managed was used to launch BitPaymer ransomware attacks against 72 U.S. companies. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: PolyShell attacks target 56% of all vulnerable Magento stores
item.description:
Attacks leveraging the 'PolyShell' vulnerability in version 2 of Magento Open Source and Adobe Commerce installations are underway, targeting more than half of all vulnerable stores. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Dixon Electrical Systems & Contracting
